title: Valve confirms premature overheating warning on Steam Machine and raises threshold to 100°C
summary: The company will release a BIOS update to fix the red LED alert that was triggering earlier than necessary, according to multiple reports.

title: A Zotac RTX 5090 catches fire during use and rules out 16-pin connector as cause
summary: Two independent reports document the incident with the high-end GPU as the real cause of the failure is investigated.

title: Twelve US states sue to block Paramount-Warner Bros. merger
summary: The $110 billion deal faces criticism from New York's mayor and a lawsuit led by state attorneys general.

title: LAPD ends contract with Flock license plate reader company over privacy concerns
summary: The Los Angeles police department did not renew the agreement following reports of wrongful arrests based on erroneous data.

title: Samsung threatens to delete health data of users who do not agree to train its AI
summary: The Samsung Health app will condition access to personal information on consent for the development of artificial intelligence models.

title: Intel unveils Starfire, an 8-core space processor based on Panther Lake architecture
summary: The chip designed for the US government uses Intel 3 technology for the GPU and will be ready for orbital missions.

title: Apple M7 Ultra rumored to contain up to 1.5 TB of unified memory and Blackwell-class performance
summary: Apple's future chip for 2028 would be a monster of capacity, according to industry reports.

title: AMD leaks its multi-frame generation technology with up to 8x in its drivers
summary: AMD's experimental drivers suggest that the next version of FSR will include drastic improvements in image scaling.

title: Nearly 200 economists and Nobel laureates warn about massive labor displacement by AI
summary: The letter "We Must Act Now" signed by figures like Jack Clark of Anthropic calls for urgent measures in the face of the economic impact of artificial intelligence.

title: Anthropic extends free access to Claude Fable 5 until July 19
summary: The extension of access comes amid an intense price war with OpenAI over their language models.

title: SpaceX receives authorization to resume Starship flights after booster failure in May
summary: The FAA has given the green light to the company to resume testing of the megrocket, after the booster failure in the last launch.

title: Meta removes its AI-generated image feature on Instagram after user criticism
summary: The company decided to remove the tool after a wave of complaints about the use of artificial intelligence on the platform.

title: Satya Nadella warns companies that uncontrolled use of AI can be harmful
summary: Microsoft's CEO urged companies to be cautious with the implementation of artificial intelligence.

title: Video generation startup PixVerse raises $439 million and doubles valuation
summary: The two-year-old company reaches a valuation of over $2 billion after this funding round.

title: General Fusion goes public as the first nuclear fusion company to list on the stock exchange
summary: The nuclear fusion company received strong backing from investors in its IPO, marking a milestone for the sector.

title: Turing Award winner Rich Sutton founds Oak Lab to develop AI agents that learn by themselves
summary: The lab will focus on creating artificial intelligence that learns autonomously, without human supervision.

title: Telegram's t.me domain has been temporarily suspended
summary: The suspension of the messaging platform's link shortener has generated uncertainty among users.

title: Oracle plummets 50% since June and knocks Larry Ellison out of the top 10 billionaires
summary: The sharp stock market decline of the software company has erased billions from its founder's fortune.

title: New York will ban deceptive subscription practices and require easy cancellations
summary: The new city law seeks to protect consumers from unwanted charges and complicated cancellation processes.

title: China successfully recovers first stage of a space rocket, a feat only achieved by SpaceX and Blue Origin
summary: The Asian country successfully performed the controlled landing of the first stage of its rocket, demonstrating advances in reusability.
